scene.description: The same street, twice, at once. Two versions of one night overlap. Examine across both and the reconstruction fights you.

fragments.label: Version A — Calm Before
fragments.text: Version A: people stand, talking. The panic arrives slowly, like weather. There was time.

fragments.label: Version A — Escalation
fragments.text: Version A: the calm curdles. Whatever caused it, it built. It had a beginning.

fragments.label: Version B — Immediate Chaos
fragments.text: Version B: chaos from the first frame. No build, no warning. As if the cause was edited out and the effect left running.

fragments.label: Version B — No Cause
fragments.text: Version B: you look for the start and there isn't one. The event begins already happening.

fragments.label: The Missing Cause
fragments.symbol: Between both versions, where the cause should sit: the same absence as before. Something was removed here, on purpose, twice.

fragments.label: The Witness on Both Sides
fragments.text: One figure stands in both versions, unmoved by either. In A they're a bystander. In B they're the only one not running. Same person, both nights.

fragments.label: The Clock
fragments.text: A clock visible in both versions reads the same time — but the light says A is dusk and B is deep night. The hour was edited; the time was not.

interpretations.label: Accept Version A as true (it had a cause)
Accept Version B as true (the cause was removed)
Refuse to choose — let both stand
